cases.description: Differential VCO With Clip And Idtmod: keeps the sin() terms but multiplies them by zero so both differential arms are flat at the common mode.
Differential VCO With Clip And Idtmod: keeps the differential sine but drives the wrapped-phase metric to zero so the metric trace fails.
Differential VCO With Clip And Idtmod: uses a 0.5 idtmod modulus so the phase wraps twice per cycle and the sine and metric diverge from the modulo-1 reference.
Differential VCO With Clip And Idtmod: flips the dFdV sign so the frequency moves the wrong way with the differential control and the reintegrated phase diverges.
Differential VCO With Clip And Idtmod: drives both arms with the same polarity so outp and outm no longer bracket the common mode symmetrically.
